function.name: skill_manage
function.arguments: {"action":"patch","name":"verified-algebra-workflow","new_string":"  - **Numeric-anchor reproducibility gate before refactoring/aligning (updated 2026-07-14):** Independently reproduce a cited number from the actual artifact—never from a docstring or summary. The live repo's degenerate `extract_mixing` path and all 32 Stage-H frame-overlap checkpoints still do **not** reproduce the historical `~1e-5` number (best Stage-H overlap fro `0.32`), so do not align either of those paths to it. However, the distinct archived Hermes general-FTS pipeline has now been recovered with `Q_star,J_u,J_d,r,s` and independently reruns Frobenius `6.1281124e-6`; use that lock as the historical diagnostic artifact. This is not a contradiction: these are different extraction paths. Reproducibility upgrades the number to a verified fit, not to a derivation. See `references/ckm-anchor-reproducibility-gate.md` and `references/locked-historical-fts-fit-recovery-2026-07-14.md`.","old_string":"  - **Phantom numeric anchor — reproducibility gate before refactoring/aligning (caught 2026-07-12):** Before you refactor or \"align\" code to a cited number (e.g. \"fro~1e-5 CKM\"), **independently reproduce it from the actual artifact** — do NOT take it from a docstring, comment, or summary. In this project fro~1e-5 was unreproducible in *both* candidate paths: (a) moment-map `extract_mixing` is degenerate (image-dim 3, cannot encode 3 angles — see `fts-reconstruct-h-degeneracy.md`); (b) stage_h frame-overlap `abs(Q[0].T@Q[1])` scanned across **all 32 real `.pt` checkpoints** gave best fro = **0.32**, zero below 2e-5. **Rule: if a cited anchor cannot be reproduced from the artifact, the gate FAILS and you must NOT align/refactor to it (that builds infra on a false premise). Report the negative gate; record the best-achieved value as a diagnostic floor, explicitly not a derivation.** Reusable scan recipe in `references/ckm-anchor-reproducibility-gate.md`. Even an explicit \"GO FOR IT / align to X\" authorizes the *verified plan*, not skipping the premise check — executing a refactor onto a phantom anchor is fabrication-adjacent."}

parsed_arguments.name: verified-algebra-workflow
parsed_arguments.new_string: - **Numeric-anchor reproducibility gate before refactoring/aligning (updated 2026-07-14):** Independently reproduce a cited number from the actual artifact—never from a docstring or summary. The live repo's degenerate `extract_mixing` path and all 32 Stage-H frame-overlap checkpoints still do **not** reproduce the historical `~1e-5` number (best Stage-H overlap fro `0.32`), so do not align either of those paths to it. However, the distinct archived Hermes general-FTS pipeline has now been recovered with `Q_star,J_u,J_d,r,s` and independently reruns Frobenius `6.1281124e-6`; use that lock as the historical diagnostic artifact. This is not a contradiction: these are different extraction paths. Reproducibility upgrades the number to a verified fit, not to a derivation. See `references/ckm-anchor-reproducibility-gate.md` and `references/locked-historical-fts-fit-recovery-2026-07-14.md`.
parsed_arguments.old_string: - **Phantom numeric anchor — reproducibility gate before refactoring/aligning (caught 2026-07-12):** Before you refactor or "align" code to a cited number (e.g. "fro~1e-5 CKM"), **independently reproduce it from the actual artifact** — do NOT take it from a docstring, comment, or summary. In this project fro~1e-5 was unreproducible in *both* candidate paths: (a) moment-map `extract_mixing` is degenerate (image-dim 3, cannot encode 3 angles — see `fts-reconstruct-h-degeneracy.md`); (b) stage_h frame-overlap `abs(Q[0].T@Q[1])` scanned across **all 32 real `.pt` checkpoints** gave best fro = **0.32**, zero below 2e-5. **Rule: if a cited anchor cannot be reproduced from the artifact, the gate FAILS and you must NOT align/refactor to it (that builds infra on a false premise). Report the negative gate; record the best-achieved value as a diagnostic floor, explicitly not a derivation.** Reusable scan recipe in `references/ckm-anchor-reproducibility-gate.md`. Even an explicit "GO FOR IT / align to X" authorizes the *verified plan*, not skipping the premise check — executing a refactor onto a phantom anchor is fabrication-adjacent.
